source_meta.dialect: Palestinian Arabic (Gaza Strip; mixed urban realizations with phonological variation)
source_meta.collection_context_en: A young girl recounts the death of her father and multiple family members, describing what she witnessed and her longing to see her father again. The testimony includes crying and a prolonged silence.
source_meta.collection_context_ar: طفلة تروي استشهاد والدها وعدد من أفراد عائلتها، وتصف ما رأته بعينيها وشوقها الشديد لوالدها. تتخلل الرواية لحظات صمت وبكاء.

speaker_profile.origin: Gaza Strip (exact locality not disclosed for anonymization)

approx_context.brief_summary_en: A child describes losing her father and multiple relatives, focusing on her longing to see her father and the loss of the household’s main provider, followed by crying and silence.
approx_context.brief_summary_ar: طفلة تصف فقدان والدها وعددًا كبيرًا من أقاربها، وتتمحور روايتها حول شوقها لرؤية والدها وفقدان سند البيت، ثم تنتهي بصمت وبكاء.

segments.text_ar: بدي أقلك اشي واحد يا عمو، أنا أبويا است.. مات من رفح من يوم الاتنين اللي فات فات. أنا كنت... نفسي أشوفه بس، وهو كان نفسه يشوفنا.
segments.text_en: I want to tell you one thing, uncle: my father… died in Rafah last Monday. I just wanted to see him, and he also wanted to see us.

event_frame.adversity_description: Recent death of the father (Rafah referenced), with unfulfilled mutual desire for a final reunion.
event_frame.response_description: Direct expression of longing and loss, with hesitation consistent with emotional overload.

affective_signals.justification: The speaker repeatedly expresses longing to see her father, with noticeable hesitation and broken phrasing.

discourse_markers.note: Interrupted phrasing (است.. مات) and pauses indicate difficulty continuing.

segments.text_ar: وأنا شفت قدّام عيني إخواتي وهو مرميّين ع الأرض. لأنه أنا شفتهم إخواتي بس كان نفسي أشوف أبويا.
segments.text_en: I saw, right in front of me, my siblings and him lying on the ground. I saw my siblings, but I still wished I could see my father.

event_frame.adversity_description: Witnessing close family members lying on the ground in the immediate aftermath of genocide.
event_frame.response_description: Fragmented recounting with pronoun confusion while prioritising longing for the father.

affective_signals.justification: Graphic witnessing combined with disorganised phrasing and repeated ‘I saw’ signals acute shock.

dialect_markers.note: Often realised with glottal stop (Hamza instead of Qaf) in some Gaza female speech; common spoken form.

discourse_markers.note: ‘هو مرميّين’ reflects speech disruption under distress (not corrected to preserve verbatim authenticity).

segments.text_ar: راح من عنا عشر شُهَدا. وكان نفسي أشوف بالزات أبويا. هو اللي كان يصرف علينا، هو كان كل البيت.
segments.text_en: We lost ten martyrs from our family. And I especially wished I could see my father. He was the one who provided for us—he was the whole house.

event_frame.adversity_description: Large-scale family loss (ten deaths) and the loss of the household’s primary provider.
event_frame.response_description: Meaning-making through framing the father as the household’s core support and safety.

affective_signals.justification: The father is described as ‘the whole house,’ indicating attachment, safety, and profound loss.

religious_markers.note: Common religious-communal framing for war death in Gaza narratives.

segments.text_ar: كان كلهم يقولوا: فش إلا ضل [اسم محذوف لأسباب تتعلق بإغفاء الهوية]، بس يخلص الحرب إحنا بنشوفه، وهي ربنا أخده. بس نفسي كان أشوفه.
segments.text_en: They used to say: ‘No one is left except No one is left except [Name redacted for anonymization]; once the war ends, we’ll see him.’ But then Allah took him. I still just wanted to see him.

event_frame.adversity_description: Loss of the remaining hoped-for family member after expectations of reunion post-war.
event_frame.response_description: Hope is expressed and then collapses, framed through religious language (‘Allah took him’) - meaning that he died later.

affective_signals.justification: The sequence of anticipated reunion followed by loss signals collapse of hope.

religious_markers.note: Religious framing of death (Allah’s decree), often used for endurance amid loss.

dialect_markers.note: Spoken Gaza form meaning 'there is no'.

segments.text_ar:
الصحفي: يعني انتي أبوكي استشهد؟
البنت: آه، وإخواتي وأعمامي وعمتي وابن عمتي كلهم استشهدوا. فش النا أي حدّا في الضار.
segments.text_en:
Journalist: So your father was martyred?
Girl: Yes—and my siblings, my uncles, my aunt, and my cousin all were martyred. We have no one left in the house.

event_frame.adversity_description: Confirmation of multiple family deaths and perceived total family disappearance from the home.
event_frame.response_description: Direct confirmation, using cumulative listing to convey scale of loss.

affective_signals.justification: Listing many losses in a single breath reflects overwhelm and emotional saturation.

dialect_markers.note: Spoken structure expressing total absence of support.

segments.text_ar:
الصحفي: انتي عند مين عايشة هلقيت؟
البنت: أنا عند أهلي، عند إمي وأخويا. جوز أختي استشهد، وهي أختي عنا برضو، وأولادها كلهم مصّاوبين.
segments.text_en: Journalist: Who are you living with now? Girl: I’m with my family—with my mother and my brother. My sister’s husband was martyred, my sister is with us too, and all her children are injured.

event_frame.adversity_description: Disrupted living situation and continued injuries within the extended family network.
event_frame.response_description: Shifts to current survival arrangements and caregiving burden.

affective_signals.justification: Ongoing uncertainty about living arrangements and injured children indicates sustained anxiety.

segments.text_en: Then the girl fell silent and burst into tears.

event_frame.response_description: Silence and crying function as non-verbal expression when speech becomes impossible.

affective_signals.justification: Prolonged silence and sobbing indicate intense grief beyond verbal expression.

discourse_markers.note: Silence occurring at a peak of emotional load.
Narrative ends without verbal closure, replaced by crying.
Audible crying as a paralinguistic marker of grief.

anonymization.notes: No replacements were required under the current manifest; this copy mirrors the source folder. Public-release bundle: reversibility metadata stripped for GitHub.
